title: High-fidelity iSWAP gate with Double Transmon Coupler
authors: Tarush Tiwari, Sudhir K. Sahu, Guilhem Ribeill, Michael Senatore, Matthew D. LaHaye, Raymond W. Simmonds, Daniel L. Campbell, Archana Kamal, Leonardo Ranzani
abstract: Entangling operations are at the heart of all approaches to quantum information processing. Parametric gates, in particular, offer a versatile solution to strongly couple off-resonant superconducting qubits with suppressed parasitic crosstalk to spectator qubits due to frequency-selective activation. In this work, we demonstrate a parametric iSWAP gate between two transmon qubits using the recently developed double transmon coupler (DTC). The DTC supports robust internally-defined cancellation point (``off'' state) for static interactions, while simultaneously mediating a fast parametric coupling between data qubits that can be deployed for high-fidelity two-qubit operations. We use robust phase estimation to calibrate non-commuting error terms in the parametric iSWAP gate, and achieve a 99.827% gate fidelity in 40ns without any numerical optimization. The circuit architecture and calibration techniques developed here are extensible to other gate implementations and qubit modalities, paving the way towards resource-efficient quantum information processing.
summary: This paper demonstrates a high-fidelity 99.827% iSWAP gate between two transmon qubits using a double transmon coupler. By using parametric driving, the researchers achieved a fast 40ns gate while effectively suppressing unwanted static interactions and crosstalk. This approach provides a scalable and resource-efficient method for implementing two-qubit operations in superconducting processors.
main_problem: Achieving high-fidelity entangling gates between superconducting qubits while suppressing parasitic crosstalk and static interactions.
main_result: Demonstration of a parametric iSWAP gate with 99.827% fidelity in 40ns using a double transmon coupler.
method: The researchers used a parametric driving scheme on a double transmon coupler (DTC) and employed robust phase estimation to calibrate non-commuting error terms.
model_or_system: A superconducting circuit architecture consisting of two transmon qubits coupled via a recently developed double transmon coupler (DTC).
paper_structure: The paper introduces the problem of parasitic crosstalk, describes the DTC architecture, details the parametric iSWAP implementation and calibration via robust phase estimation, and presents the achieved fidelity results.
why_interesting: The use of parametric gates to achieve frequency-selective activation and the suppression of crosstalk is highly relevant to the control of open quantum systems and the development of scalable quantum architectures.
